method_atom: a solution-generating symmetry (a duality/triality group of algebraic transformations) that maps one solution of a nonlinear evolution equation driven by a single self-interacting degree of freedom into another, by re-expressing the state variables as functions of that degree of freedom and then interchanging or inverting them while leaving one combination invariant, generating an equivalence class of related dynamical trajectories.
object_atom: spatially flat and curved isotropic (FRW) cosmological backgrounds whose expansion is driven by an ordinary or phantom self-interacting scalar field or perfect fluids — accelerating (inflationary), contracting/decelerating (cyclic), and phantom universes — and the Randall-Sundrum type II braneworld scenario.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the algebraic duality/triality machinery that permutes and inverts the variables of a scalar-driven evolution law to generate related solutions; the domain-specific part is the FRW/braneworld cosmological system with its scale factor, Hubble parameter, and scalar-field/fluid sources.
